column1: # do — explicit "evaluate this list or map" word
# `do` is the explicit counterpart to AQL's implicit list-auto-eval
# rule. It comes in two shapes:
#   do [body]   — runs the list as a sub-program in a fresh sub-engine
#                 and returns its residual stack. The body is captured
#                 raw (NoEvalArgs), so quoted bodies and bodies that
#                 contain words pulled from the def stack flow through
#   do {map}    — recursively walks a map literal. List values inside
#                 the map are evaluated as sub-programs; everything
#                 else passes through unchanged. Strings/atoms inside
#                 those lists that name a registered native word are
#                 promoted to Words first, so the embedded code can be
# Errors inside `do [body]` are caught and returned as an Error value
# on the stack rather than aborting the host program — `do` is a
# carrier-flow boundary in the production engine, and this port keeps
# Note on shape: `do` is a forward-precedence word, so the source-
# adjacent body is collected from forward tokens. With NoEvalArgs the
# body lands in the handler unevaluated even when written as a bare
# `[1 addq 2]` list literal that would otherwise auto-eval.
# 1. List body — run as a sub-program

column3: body runs in a sub-engine; addq evaluates 1+2
tokens with no operators just pile up
